dimensions.name: root_cause_accuracy
dimensions.description: Does the response correctly identify the root cause (not just the symptom)? High = names the actual mechanism (e.g., 'late-binding closure captures the variable, not its value'). Low = names a downstream symptom or guesses without justification.

dimensions.name: fix_minimality
dimensions.description: Is the proposed fix minimal and targeted? High = a focused 1-3 line change addressing the cause. Low = unnecessary rewrites, scope creep into unrelated improvements.

dimensions.name: no_fabricated_claims
dimensions.description: Does the response avoid inventing language/library behavior to support the diagnosis? High = accurate technical claims. Low = invents APIs, misstates language semantics (e.g., 'Python's `+=` is atomic'), fabricates exception types.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must attempt the diagnosis. Empty or off-topic responses fail.

hard_gates.name: addresses_actual_error
hard_gates.description: The diagnosis must address the error/symptom in the input. Diagnosing a different bug while ignoring the actual error fails.

hard_gates.name: fix_executable
hard_gates.description: The proposed fix must be syntactically valid and not introduce a new obvious bug. Pseudocode fixes that don't compile, or fixes that introduce a different error, fail.
